user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate 3-phase complementary PWM on TOM1 at 20 kHz center-aligned with deadtime=0.5 us and min pulse=1.0 us on pins P00.3/2 (U), P00.5/4 (V), P00.7/6 (W). Start duties at U=25%, V=50%, W=75% and every 500 ms increment each by +10% with wrap in 0..100%, applying updates synchronously at the PWM period boundary.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use the unified GTM PWM driver to configure TOM1 with three complementary channel pairs and hardware dead-time, map TOUT12/11, TOUT14/13, TOUT16/15 to P00.3/2, P00.5/4, P00.7/6 via GTM PinMap, set 20 kHz center-aligned base, set deadtime=0.5 us and min pulse=1.0 us, then perform synchronous multi-channel duty updates every 500 ms using non-immediate update APIs to latch at the next period.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: It is available in the template and provides high-level multi-channel synchronized PWM with complementary pairs, dead time handling, center-aligned mode, and non-immediate update APIs to latch changes synchronously at the period boundary. This directly satisfies the advanced 3-phase inverter requirements on TOM1.

runtime_function.name: updateGtmTom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Every 500 ms compute new duty values for U, V, W by adding +10% with wrap in 0..100%, then call a single multi-channel non-immediate update so all three phase pairs latch synchronously at the next PWM period boundary.

driver_changes: Reference IfxGtm_Tom_PwmHl mapped to unified IfxGtm_Pwm for multi-channel synchronous control with dead-time
api_changes: Pin setup via IfxGtm_PinMap_setTomTout on TC38x LFBGA-516 package
migration_notes.context_preserved: TOM1 and the exact pins (P00.3/2, P00.5/4, P00.7/6) are retained using the GTM TOM TOUT pin map. Deadtime=0.5 us and min pulse=1.0 us are applied via the unified PWM driver with complementary pairing. The PWM is configured center-aligned at 20 kHz. Initial duties are set to U=25%, V=50%, W=75%, and a single multi-channel non-immediate update API is used every 500 ms to increment duties by +10% with wrap, ensuring synchronous application at the period boundary.
